requirements.requirement: Establish a Physical Security Policy for customer information that covers both paper and electronic records, defining protection requirements for all premises where customer information is handled or stored.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.28 requires physical security for both forms. Physical compromise can bypass all logical security controls.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Physical security technology must protect both paper document storage areas and electronic equipment hosting customer information.

requirements.requirement: Deploy physical security technology (access card systems, CCTV, alarm systems, environmental controls) at all locations where customer information is stored or processed in paper or electronic form.
requirements.rationale: Physical security technology provides deterrence, detection, and forensic evidence for physical compromise attempts.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Physical security controls must be subject to regular assessment and testing.

requirements.requirement: Conduct annual physical security assessments of all premises where customer information is handled, stored, or processed.
requirements.rationale: Physical security requires periodic validation to identify deterioration or gaps.

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must restrict access and deploy intruder deterrents in areas storing large volumes of or critical and sensitive customer information, such as server rooms and filing rooms.

requirements.requirement: Identify and classify areas where large amounts or critical/sensitive customer information is accessible or stored, and implement enhanced access restrictions and intruder deterrents at these locations.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.29 requires enhanced protection for high-risk physical areas. Classification ensures proportionate protection.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Enhanced physical security technology must be deployed at high-risk areas.

requirements.requirement: Implement enhanced physical access controls (biometric access, mantrap entries, visitor escort requirements) and intruder detection systems (motion sensors, alarm systems) at classified high-risk areas such as server rooms and filing rooms.
requirements.rationale: High-risk areas require stronger physical controls than general office areas.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Access to high-risk areas must be logged and regularly reviewed.

requirements.requirement: Maintain access logs for all classified high-risk areas and review them periodically for unauthorised or unusual access patterns.
requirements.rationale: Logging and review provide both deterrence and detection for physical access to sensitive areas.

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs are encouraged to implement a clear-desk policy to reduce the risk of customer information being compromised through physical exposure.

requirements.requirement: Implement and enforce a clear-desk policy requiring staff to secure all customer information documents and remove them from desks when unattended or at end of day.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.30 recommends a clear-desk policy. This simple control significantly reduces exposure of customer information to unauthorised viewing or theft.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technology controls should complement the clear-desk policy with automatic screen locks and secure printing.

requirements.requirement: Implement automatic screen lock policies and secure print release (pull printing) to complement the clear-desk policy.
requirements.rationale: Screen locks prevent viewing of on-screen customer information; secure printing prevents uncollected printouts from being exposed.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Clear-desk policy compliance should be periodically verified.

requirements.requirement: Conduct periodic clear-desk compliance checks (walkthroughs) with results reported to management.
requirements.rationale: Policies require verification. Walkthroughs identify non-compliance and reinforce expected behaviours.

10.31.subsection: C: Control measures - Off-site and data-in-transit

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must have clear policies and procedures with adequate controls for handling customer information collected off-site and in-transit, including secure storage of physical documents and protection of data on portable devices.

requirements.requirement: Develop written policies and procedures for off-site customer information collection and data-in-transit scenarios, covering secure handling, storage, and transport of both physical and digital customer information.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.31 requires clear policies and procedures for off-site and in-transit data. These scenarios present elevated risks due to reduced physical controls.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technical controls must protect customer information during off-site collection and transit.

requirements.requirement: Implement encryption for all data-in-transit (TLS, VPN tunnels) and encryption at rest on all portable devices used for off-site customer information collection.
requirements.rationale: Technical encryption controls protect data during the most vulnerable phases: collection off-site and transit between locations.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Off-site collection and data-in-transit activities must be governed with approval and tracking processes.

requirements.requirement: Establish a tracking and approval process for off-site customer information collection activities, including chain of custody documentation.
requirements.rationale: Off-site activities require additional governance to compensate for reduced direct oversight.

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must have procedures to identify customer information no longer required operationally or legally, and deploy appropriate methods to securely dispose of both paper and digital records.

requirements.requirement: Develop a Data Retention and Disposal Policy that defines retention periods for each category of customer information and triggers for disposal when information is no longer needed.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.32 requires identification of information no longer required. A retention schedule provides the framework for making disposal decisions.

requirements.requirement: Establish secure disposal procedures for both paper records (shredding, incineration) and digital records (data wiping, physical destruction) of customer information.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.32 requires secure disposal of both paper and digital records. Procedures must ensure information cannot be recovered.
requirements.frequency: Annual review; executed per disposal event

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technology teams must implement secure data destruction capabilities for both digital storage and physical media.

requirements.requirement: Implement certified data destruction tools and processes for digital media including data wiping software (meeting NIST 800-88 or equivalent standards), physical destruction capabilities, and degaussing equipment as appropriate.
requirements.rationale: Secure digital disposal requires industry-standard destruction methods. Simple file deletion is insufficient.
requirements.frequency: Tools maintained continuously; used per disposal event

governance.summary: AI Generated: Disposal activities must be documented and verified through governance processes.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a Disposal Register documenting all customer information disposal events including data type, volume, disposal method, date, responsible party, and verification of complete destruction.
requirements.rationale: Documentation provides an audit trail confirming proper disposal and prevents disputes about data retention obligations.
requirements.frequency: Per disposal event; register reviewed quarterly

business.summary: AI Generated: Secure disposal means customer information is irrecoverable, irreversible, and cannot be reconstructed by any means.

requirements.requirement: Define secure disposal standards that require disposal methods to render customer information irrecoverable, irreversible, and non-reconstructable, and verify all disposal methods meet this standard.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.33 defines the standard for secure disposal. All methods must demonstrably meet this threshold.
requirements.frequency: Annual verification of disposal method effectiveness

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technical disposal methods must be validated to ensure data is truly irrecoverable.

requirements.requirement: Validate disposal methods through periodic verification testing to confirm that disposed data cannot be recovered by any means, including advanced forensic recovery techniques.
requirements.rationale: Validation ensures that disposal methods actually achieve the irrecoverability standard defined in clause 10.33.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Disposal standards must be documented and disposal method certifications maintained.

requirements.requirement: Document the FSP's secure disposal standard and maintain certifications or validation reports for each disposal method used.
requirements.rationale: Documented standards and validation evidence demonstrate regulatory compliance.

business.summary: AI Generated: Devices should be sanitised using industry-standard methods (data wiping, physical destruction, degaussing) with chain of custody controls. Simple deletion or reformatting is insufficient for digital devices.

requirements.requirement: Adopt industry-standard sanitisation methods for all devices containing customer information, and explicitly prohibit simple file deletion or reformatting as sole disposal methods.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.34 warns that simple deletion/reformatting is insufficient. Industry-standard methods must be mandated.
requirements.frequency: Per disposal event; standards reviewed annually

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technology teams must deploy industry-standard sanitisation tools and maintain chain of custody controls.

requirements.requirement: Deploy NIST 800-88 compliant data wiping software, maintain physical destruction capabilities (shredders, crushers), and establish chain of custody tracking for devices awaiting or undergoing sanitisation.
requirements.rationale: Industry-standard tools ensure effective sanitisation. Chain of custody prevents data exposure between decommissioning and destruction.
requirements.frequency: Tools maintained continuously; chain of custody per device

governance.summary: AI Generated: Sanitisation records and chain of custody documentation must be maintained.

requirements.requirement: Maintain sanitisation certificates and chain of custody records for all devices containing customer information that undergo disposal or decommissioning.
requirements.rationale: Records provide evidence that proper sanitisation occurred and maintain accountability throughout the disposal process.
requirements.frequency: Per device; records retained per retention policy

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must conduct a risk-benefit assessment before engaging outsourced service providers for customer information destruction that involves transporting data off-premises.

requirements.requirement: Conduct a formal risk-benefit assessment before engaging any outsourced service provider for customer information destruction, documenting the risks of off-premises transport against the benefits of specialised destruction services.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.35 mandates this assessment. Transporting customer information off-premises for destruction introduces additional risk that must be weighed against benefits.
requirements.frequency: Per OSP engagement; reviewed at contract renewal

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technology controls should support tracking and monitoring of customer information sent for outsourced destruction.

requirements.requirement: Implement tracking systems for customer information materials sent for outsourced destruction, including barcode/RFID tracking of containers and GPS tracking of transport vehicles where proportionate.
requirements.rationale: Tracking provides visibility into the disposal chain and enables verification that materials reach the destruction facility.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Risk-benefit assessments must be documented and approved by appropriate management.

requirements.requirement: Document and obtain senior management approval for risk-benefit assessments of outsourced destruction arrangements, with periodic reassessment.
requirements.rationale: Management approval ensures the risk acceptance decision is made at an appropriate level.

business.summary: AI Generated: Before collection by outsourced service providers for destruction, customer information must be shredded or stored inaccessibly in tamper-proof sealed bags or locked containers.

requirements.requirement: Establish pre-collection security procedures requiring that customer information is either shredded or sealed in tamper-proof bags or locked containers before being handed to outsourced destruction service providers.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.36 mandates pre-collection protection. This prevents exposure of customer information during the collection and transport process.
requirements.frequency: Per collection event; procedures reviewed annually

technology.summary: AI Generated: Secure storage facilities and tamper-evident packaging must be available at all locations generating customer information waste.

requirements.requirement: Provide tamper-proof sealed bags, locked collection containers, and on-site shredding equipment at all locations where customer information documents are generated for disposal.
requirements.rationale: Physical security equipment must be readily available to ensure pre-collection protection is practical and consistently applied.
requirements.frequency: Equipment maintained continuously; availability audited annually

governance.summary: AI Generated: Pre-collection procedures must be documented and compliance verified.

requirements.requirement: Verify compliance with pre-collection security procedures through periodic spot checks and handover documentation review.
requirements.rationale: Procedures are only effective if consistently followed. Spot checks ensure compliance.

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must perform random checks on outsourced service providers' collection and destruction processes to verify proper destruction of customer information.

requirements.requirement: Implement a random inspection programme for outsourced destruction service providers, including unannounced site visits and observation of collection and destruction processes.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.37 mandates random checks. Random inspections verify that destruction providers maintain standards consistently, not just during planned audits.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technology may support verification through destruction certificates, video evidence, and audit trail systems.

requirements.requirement: Require outsourced destruction providers to supply certificates of destruction and, where feasible, video evidence of destruction for each batch of customer information destroyed.
requirements.rationale: Certificates and video evidence provide verifiable proof of destruction complementing random physical checks.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Random check results must be documented and non-compliance addressed.

requirements.requirement: Document all random check findings and escalate any non-compliance or concerns to senior management for action, including consideration of alternative providers for serious deficiencies.
requirements.rationale: Inspection results must drive corrective action. Documentation provides the evidence base for provider management decisions.

10.38.subsection: D: Staff, Representatives, Agents and External Vendors' Personnel

business.summary: AI Generated: Human factors are a common cause of customer information compromise. All staff must understand the importance of protecting customer information confidentiality and security.

requirements.requirement: Integrate human factor risk considerations into the customer information risk assessment, identifying high-risk roles, processes, and scenarios where human error or misconduct is most likely.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.38 highlights human factors as common causes. Risk assessments must explicitly address the human element.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technology controls should compensate for human factor risks through automation and error-proofing.

requirements.requirement: Implement technology controls that reduce reliance on human diligence, such as automated data masking, confirmation prompts before bulk data operations, and DLP rules that prevent accidental mass disclosures.
requirements.rationale: Technology controls compensate for human fallibility by preventing common error scenarios.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Human factor risks should be addressed through governance measures including awareness programmes and cultural initiatives.

requirements.requirement: Include human factor risk analysis in governance reporting to senior management, tracking trends in human-caused incidents and effectiveness of mitigation measures.
requirements.rationale: Governance visibility into human factor trends enables targeted interventions.

10.39.subsection: D: Staff, Representatives, Agents and External Vendors' Personnel

business.summary: AI Generated: Employment contracts must include a confidentiality undertaking signed by all staff, specifying legal obligations to safeguard customer information and consequences for non-compliance.

requirements.requirement: Include a Customer Information Confidentiality Undertaking in all employment contracts that specifies the legal obligations under relevant written law to safeguard customer information and clearly states the consequences for failure to comply.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.39 mandates contractual confidentiality undertakings. This creates a legally binding obligation and ensures staff are aware of consequences.
requirements.frequency: At hiring; reviewed annually for adequacy

requirements.requirement: Ensure all existing staff who have not signed the confidentiality undertaking are required to do so, with records maintained centrally.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.39 applies to all staff. Legacy employees must also be covered.
requirements.frequency: One-time remediation; ongoing for new hires

technology.summary: AI Generated: HR systems should track confidentiality undertaking signatures for all staff.

requirements.requirement: Configure HR systems to track and report on confidentiality undertaking signature status for all staff, flagging any staff without signed undertakings.
requirements.rationale: Automated tracking ensures no staff member is missed and provides compliance evidence.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Confidentiality undertaking coverage must be monitored and reported.

requirements.requirement: Monitor confidentiality undertaking coverage percentage and report any gaps to senior management, with a target of 100% coverage.
requirements.rationale: Complete coverage is a regulatory requirement. Monitoring ensures compliance.

10.40.subsection: D: Staff, Representatives, Agents and External Vendors' Personnel

business.summary: AI Generated: When engaging external vendors working on-premises (e.g., security guards, cleaners, maintenance staff), FSPs must ensure vendors appropriately vet and monitor their personnel to mitigate customer information theft risk.

requirements.requirement: Require all on-premises external vendors to demonstrate that they conduct appropriate vetting (background checks, reference checks) on personnel who may have access to areas containing customer information.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.40 requires FSPs to ensure external vendor personnel are vetted. On-premises vendors can access physical areas containing customer information.

requirements.requirement: Require external vendors to implement ongoing monitoring of their on-premises personnel, including supervision requirements and incident reporting obligations.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.40 requires both vetting and monitoring. Ongoing monitoring addresses risks that emerge after initial vetting.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Physical access systems should support vendor personnel access management and tracking.

requirements.requirement: Issue time-limited, area-restricted access credentials to external vendor personnel, with access logging and automatic expiration.
requirements.rationale: Technical access controls limit vendor personnel to necessary areas and provide audit trails.
requirements.frequency: Per vendor personnel; access reviewed monthly

governance.summary: AI Generated: Vendor vetting and monitoring requirements must be contractually documented and compliance verified.

requirements.requirement: Include vetting and monitoring requirements in all on-premises vendor contracts, with the right to verify compliance through audit and inspection.
requirements.rationale: Contractual obligations provide the legal basis for enforcing vetting and monitoring requirements on external vendors.
requirements.frequency: At contract execution; reviewed at renewal

10.41.subsection: D: Staff, Representatives, Agents and External Vendors' Personnel

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must maintain high staff awareness at all times on: protecting customer information, complying with policies, and understanding the consequences of involvement in any breach.

requirements.requirement: Implement a continuous Staff Awareness Programme addressing three mandatory areas: (a) importance of protecting customer information confidentiality and security, (b) compliance with policies and procedures, and (c) consequences of involvement in any breach.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.41 specifies three mandatory awareness areas that must be maintained at all times. A continuous programme ensures sustained awareness.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Awareness delivery should leverage technology platforms for reach and tracking.

requirements.requirement: Utilise e-learning platforms, desktop reminders, login banners, and digital signage to deliver and reinforce customer information protection awareness messages continuously.
requirements.rationale: Technology-enabled delivery ensures reach across the organisation and provides usage tracking.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Awareness programme effectiveness must be measured and reported.

requirements.requirement: Measure staff awareness levels through periodic assessments (quizzes, surveys) and report results to senior management, with targeted interventions for low-scoring areas.
requirements.rationale: Awareness programmes must demonstrate effectiveness. Measurement enables targeted improvement.

10.42.subsection: D: Staff, Representatives, Agents and External Vendors' Personnel

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must implement robust monitoring mechanisms to ensure staff adhere to established policies, procedures, and controls.

requirements.requirement: Implement a staff compliance monitoring programme with defined monitoring activities, frequency, and escalation procedures for identified non-compliance.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.42 requires robust monitoring of staff adherence. Monitoring provides assurance that policies are followed in practice.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technology must support automated monitoring of staff compliance with customer information protection policies.

requirements.requirement: Deploy automated compliance monitoring tools that track staff adherence to customer information protection controls (e.g., DLP policy violations, access control exceptions, data handling procedure deviations).
requirements.rationale: Automated monitoring provides continuous, scalable compliance assurance beyond what manual oversight can achieve.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Monitoring results must be reported through governance channels with consequences for persistent non-compliance.

requirements.requirement: Report staff compliance monitoring results to senior management quarterly, with trend analysis and escalation of persistent or significant non-compliance.
requirements.rationale: Governance oversight of monitoring results ensures that non-compliance is addressed and systemic issues are identified.

10.43.subsection: D: Staff, Representatives, Agents and External Vendors' Personnel

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must provide relevant training and regularly remind all staff about their obligations to safeguard customer information confidentiality.

requirements.requirement: Deliver role-specific customer information protection training to all staff and supplement with regular reminders (e.g., email bulletins, posters, team briefings) about confidentiality obligations.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.43 requires both training and regular reminders. Training provides depth; reminders maintain top-of-mind awareness.
requirements.frequency: Training annually; reminders at least quarterly

technology.summary: AI Generated: Training delivery and reminder systems must reach all staff effectively.

requirements.requirement: Utilise LMS for formal training delivery and enterprise communication tools for regular reminder distribution, with delivery and engagement tracking.
requirements.rationale: Systematic delivery ensures universal coverage and provides evidence of compliance.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Training delivery and reminder frequency must be tracked and reported.

requirements.requirement: Track and report training completion rates and reminder delivery metrics, escalating gaps in coverage to management.
requirements.rationale: Tracking ensures the training and reminder obligations are met across the entire staff population.

10.44.subsection: D: Staff, Representatives, Agents and External Vendors' Personnel

business.summary: AI Generated: New staff onboarding programmes must include specific training on the FSP's policies and procedures for protecting customer information.

requirements.requirement: Include mandatory customer information protection training as a required component of all new staff onboarding programmes, to be completed before the staff member is granted access to customer information.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.44 mandates onboarding-specific training. Training before access ensures new staff understand obligations from day one.
requirements.frequency: Per new hire; content reviewed annually

technology.summary: AI Generated: Onboarding training must be integrated with access provisioning to ensure training is completed before access is granted.

requirements.requirement: Integrate onboarding training completion with access provisioning systems so that customer information system access is only granted after training completion is confirmed.
requirements.rationale: Technical integration enforces the training-before-access principle and prevents procedural bypass.
requirements.frequency: Per new hire; integration tested annually

governance.summary: AI Generated: Onboarding training completion must be verified before access is granted.

requirements.requirement: Maintain records of onboarding training completion for all new staff and verify no access is provisioned prior to training completion.
requirements.rationale: Records demonstrate compliance and the access verification ensures the control is effective.

10.45.subsection: D: Staff, Representatives, Agents and External Vendors' Personnel

business.summary: AI Generated: New staff must be informed of the possible disciplinary or other actions that may result from non-compliance with customer information protection policies and procedures.

requirements.requirement: Include a clear explanation of consequences for non-compliance (disciplinary action, termination, legal proceedings) in new staff onboarding materials and require written acknowledgement.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.45 requires notification of possible actions for non-compliance. Awareness of consequences strengthens deterrence.
requirements.frequency: Per new hire; content reviewed annually

technology.summary: AI Generated: Acknowledgement of consequences must be digitally captured and tracked.

requirements.requirement: Capture digital acknowledgements from new staff confirming they have been informed of non-compliance consequences, stored in the HR system.
requirements.rationale: Digital capture provides verifiable evidence that the notification obligation was met.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Acknowledgement records must be maintained for compliance evidence.

requirements.requirement: Monitor acknowledgement completion rates for new staff consequence notifications and escalate any gaps.
requirements.rationale: 100% acknowledgement ensures all new staff are aware of consequences as required.

10.46.subsection: D: Staff, Representatives, Agents and External Vendors' Personnel

business.summary: AI Generated: Staff guidance on safeguarding customer information should be concise and easy to understand to promote effective compliance.

requirements.requirement: Develop concise, reader-friendly staff guidance materials (quick reference cards, infographics, FAQs) that complement detailed policies and enable practical understanding of customer information protection requirements.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.46 emphasises readability and understanding. Accessible guidance promotes actual compliance.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Guidance materials should be easily accessible through technology platforms.

requirements.requirement: Publish staff guidance materials on the intranet, mobile apps, or other readily accessible platforms with search functionality and contextual linking.
requirements.rationale: Accessibility maximises the value of reader-friendly guidance.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Guidance material usability should be assessed through staff feedback.

requirements.requirement: Collect periodic staff feedback on the clarity and usability of customer information protection guidance materials and use findings to improve content.
requirements.rationale: Feedback-driven improvement ensures guidance remains practical and effective.

10.47.subsection: D: Staff, Representatives, Agents and External Vendors' Personnel

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must have mechanisms to measure the effectiveness of staff training on safeguarding customer information.

requirements.requirement: Implement training effectiveness measurement mechanisms including post-training assessments, practical scenario testing, and correlation analysis between training completion and incident rates.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.47 mandates effectiveness measurement. Training that does not change behaviour is not effective.

technology.summary: AI Generated: LMS and assessment tools should support training effectiveness measurement.

requirements.requirement: Configure LMS to deliver post-training assessments with scoring, track pass/fail rates, and generate effectiveness reports by department and role.
requirements.rationale: Automated assessment and reporting provides scalable effectiveness measurement.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Training effectiveness results must be reported and used to drive improvements.

requirements.requirement: Report training effectiveness metrics to senior management with recommendations for improvement where effectiveness is below target thresholds.
requirements.rationale: Governance oversight ensures training effectiveness is treated as a management priority.

10.48.subsection: D: Staff, Representatives, Agents and External Vendors' Personnel

business.summary: AI Generated: Training effectiveness mechanisms may include annual awareness surveys to assess staff understanding of customer information protection and breach reporting.

requirements.requirement: Conduct annual awareness surveys to assess staff understanding of customer information protection requirements and breach reporting procedures.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.48 specifically suggests annual surveys. Surveys provide a broad measure of awareness levels across the organisation.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Survey tools should support anonymous, organisation-wide awareness assessments.

requirements.requirement: Deploy survey tools that support anonymous, organisation-wide awareness assessments with automated analysis and reporting capabilities.
requirements.rationale: Anonymous surveys encourage honest responses. Automated analysis enables trend tracking over time.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Survey results should inform training programme improvements.

requirements.requirement: Analyse annual awareness survey results and use findings to update training content, target specific awareness gaps, and report trends to senior management.
requirements.rationale: Surveys are only valuable if results drive action. Governance ensures findings translate into programme improvements.

10.49.subsection: D: Staff, Representatives, Agents and External Vendors' Personnel

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must conduct thorough and timely investigations when staff are involved in customer information breaches and take appropriate actions against those involved.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Staff Breach Investigation Procedure defining investigation triggers, methodology, timeline requirements, evidence preservation, and the range of appropriate actions for staff involved in customer information breaches.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.49 requires thorough and timely investigation. A defined procedure ensures consistent and comprehensive response.
requirements.frequency: Procedure reviewed annually; executed per incident

technology.summary: AI Generated: Digital forensic and evidence preservation capabilities must support staff breach investigations.

requirements.requirement: Maintain digital forensic investigation capabilities including evidence preservation tools, audit log analysis, and chain of custody procedures for digital evidence.
requirements.rationale: Thorough investigation of digital breaches requires forensic capabilities. Proper evidence handling supports potential disciplinary or legal proceedings.
requirements.frequency: Capabilities maintained continuously; deployed per incident

governance.summary: AI Generated: Investigation procedures must ensure fairness, thoroughness, and timeliness.

requirements.requirement: Track investigation timelines against targets and report completion metrics to senior management, ensuring timeliness is maintained.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.49 requires timely investigation. Tracking prevents investigations from stalling.

10.50.subsection: D: Staff, Representatives, Agents and External Vendors' Personnel

business.summary: AI Generated: Disciplinary actions must serve as a strong deterrent. If an FSP chooses not to act against a staff member in breach, the rationale must be documented and approved by senior management.

requirements.requirement: Ensure disciplinary actions for customer information breaches are communicated organisation-wide (in anonymised form) to serve as a deterrent and send a strong message against future breaches.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.50 requires actions to send a strong message and act as a deterrent. Communication of consequences reinforces the deterrent effect.

requirements.requirement: Where the FSP decides not to take action against a staff member involved in a breach, document the rationale and obtain senior management approval, maintaining the record for audit purposes.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.50 explicitly requires documentation and senior management approval for decisions not to act. This prevents inconsistent enforcement.
requirements.frequency: Per decision; records retained per policy

technology.summary: AI Generated: Case management systems should support investigation outcomes and disciplinary action tracking.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a case management system that tracks breach investigations, disciplinary decisions, and outcomes, including documented rationale for any decisions not to act.
requirements.rationale: Systematic tracking ensures compliance with documentation requirements and supports trend analysis.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Disciplinary consistency and deterrent effectiveness must be governed.

requirements.requirement: Review disciplinary action consistency across breach cases annually, ensuring proportionate and consistent treatment that maintains the deterrent effect.
requirements.rationale: Inconsistent disciplinary actions undermine deterrence and create legal risk. Annual review ensures consistency.

10.51.subsection: D: Staff, Representatives, Agents and External Vendors' Personnel

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must report investigation results and actions taken against staff to the Board, in accordance with paragraph 9.10.

requirements.requirement: Submit investigation results and staff actions to the Board as part of breach reporting, including root cause analysis, actions taken, and lessons learnt.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.51 requires Board reporting of investigation results. Board visibility ensures governance oversight of disciplinary responses.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Investigation reporting should be supported by data from case management and forensic investigation systems.

requirements.requirement: Generate Board-ready investigation summary reports from case management systems, including investigation timeline, findings, and actions taken.
requirements.rationale: Board reports must be comprehensive yet concise. Automated report generation ensures consistency and completeness.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Board reporting on investigations must follow a defined format and process.

requirements.requirement: Use a standardised Board Investigation Report Template for all breach investigation outcomes reported to the Board, ensuring completeness and consistency.
requirements.rationale: Standardised reporting ensures all required information reaches the Board in a consistent format.

10.52.subsection: D: Staff, Representatives, Agents and External Vendors' Personnel

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs retain full accountability for the conduct and actions of their appointed representatives and agents regarding any customer information breach, regardless of the direct involvement of the FSP.

requirements.requirement: Establish contractual and operational frameworks that enable the FSP to maintain accountability for representatives' and agents' conduct, including audit rights, monitoring provisions, and breach reporting obligations.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.52 creates non-delegable accountability. The FSP must have the mechanisms to exercise oversight despite the actions being performed by third parties.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technology must extend monitoring and control capabilities to representatives' and agents' activities.

requirements.requirement: Extend monitoring, logging, and alerting capabilities to cover customer information access and handling by representatives and agents, with equivalent visibility to internal staff monitoring.
requirements.rationale: Accountability requires visibility. The FSP cannot be accountable for conduct it cannot observe.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Governance must formalise the FSP's accountability for representatives and agents.

requirements.requirement: Include explicit accountability provisions in the governance framework confirming the FSP's non-delegable responsibility for representatives' and agents' customer information handling.
requirements.rationale: The governance framework must reflect this regulatory accountability to ensure it is not overlooked or informally delegated.

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must have their customer information protection policies, procedures, and controls independently reviewed at least once every two years by a function independent of business units handling customer information.

requirements.requirement: Commission an independent review of customer information protection policies, procedures, and controls at least once every two years, ensuring the reviewer is independent of business units involved in handling customer information.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.53 mandates biennial independent review. Independence ensures objectivity and credibility of findings.
requirements.frequency: At least once every two years

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technology teams must provide full access and cooperation to independent reviewers.

requirements.requirement: Provide independent reviewers with comprehensive access to systems, configurations, logs, and documentation necessary to assess the effectiveness of technical controls protecting customer information.
requirements.rationale: Independent reviewers cannot assess effectiveness without full access. Cooperation must be mandated by management.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Independent review commissioning, scope, and execution must be formally governed.

requirements.requirement: Define the independent review scope, methodology requirements, and independence criteria in a formal Terms of Engagement, ensuring coverage of all MCIPD requirements.
requirements.rationale: A well-defined scope ensures the review is comprehensive and meets regulatory expectations.

business.summary: AI Generated: The independent review must include assessment of senior management effectiveness and oversight, and the adequacy and effectiveness of customer information protection measures.

requirements.requirement: Ensure the independent review scope explicitly includes assessment of: (a) senior management effectiveness and oversight of customer information protection, and (b) the adequacy and effectiveness of all protective measures implemented.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.54 mandates two specific assessment areas. Both must be explicitly covered in the review scope.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technical evidence must be available to support the independent review's assessment of control adequacy and effectiveness.

requirements.requirement: Prepare control effectiveness evidence packages including test results, incident data, monitoring reports, and control performance metrics to support independent review assessments.
requirements.rationale: Assessment of adequacy and effectiveness requires evidence. Pre-prepared packages ensure efficient review execution.

governance.summary: AI Generated: The independent review must have a mandate to assess governance and management effectiveness, not just technical controls.

requirements.requirement: Ensure the independent reviewer has a mandate and access to assess senior management oversight effectiveness, including reviewing Board reports, management responses, and governance meeting minutes.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.54 requires assessment of senior management effectiveness. The reviewer must have access to governance evidence.

business.summary: AI Generated: The independent reviewer must provide written findings to both senior management and the Board, covering scope, methodologies, findings, evidence, and limitations.

requirements.requirement: Require the independent reviewer to deliver formal written findings to both senior management and the Board, covering review scope, methodologies used, detailed findings, evidence obtained, and any limitations.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.55 mandates written findings to both audiences. Written reports provide the permanent record and evidence of review completion.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Written findings must include technology-specific assessments of control effectiveness.

requirements.requirement: Ensure technology-related findings are presented in the independent review report with sufficient technical detail for management action and sufficient clarity for Board understanding.
requirements.rationale: Technical findings must be actionable for technology teams while being comprehensible for Board oversight.

governance.summary: AI Generated: The Board must receive and formally acknowledge the independent review findings.

requirements.requirement: Schedule formal Board presentation of independent review findings, with documented Board acknowledgement, deliberation, and any directives issued in response to findings.
requirements.rationale: Board receipt of findings must be documented. Board response demonstrates active governance oversight.

business.summary: AI Generated: Senior management must take appropriate and timely corrective actions to address any deficiencies identified in the independent review findings.

requirements.requirement: Develop a Remediation Action Plan for all deficiencies identified in the independent review, with assigned owners, timelines, resource allocation, and success criteria, approved by senior management.
requirements.rationale: Clause 10.56 requires appropriate and timely corrective actions. A structured plan ensures deficiencies are systematically addressed.
requirements.frequency: Per review cycle; progress tracked monthly

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technology teams must implement remediation actions for technical deficiencies identified in the review.

requirements.requirement: Implement technical remediation actions for all technology-related deficiencies identified in the independent review, with testing to confirm deficiency resolution.
requirements.rationale: Identified deficiencies must be resolved. Testing confirms that remediation was effective.
requirements.frequency: Per finding; completion within agreed timelines

governance.summary: AI Generated: Remediation progress must be tracked and reported to the Board.

requirements.requirement: Track remediation progress against the action plan and report status to the Board quarterly until all deficiencies are resolved, with escalation of any overdue items.
requirements.rationale: Governance oversight of remediation ensures timely completion and Board awareness of outstanding deficiencies.

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must establish a breach handling and response plan covering all forms of customer information compromise, encompassing both deliberate and non-deliberate breaches.

requirements.requirement: Develop and maintain a comprehensive Customer Information Breach Handling and Response Plan covering theft, loss, misuse, unauthorised access, modification, and disclosure of customer information.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.1 mandates a breach handling and response plan. Pre-defined plans enable rapid, coordinated response when incidents occur.
requirements.frequency: Annual review; tested via simulation at least annually

requirements.requirement: Ensure the breach response plan explicitly addresses both deliberate (malicious) and non-deliberate (accidental/negligent) breach scenarios with tailored response procedures for each.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.1 specifically requires coverage of both deliberate and non-deliberate breaches. Response approaches differ based on intent.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technology infrastructure must support rapid breach detection, containment, and response execution.

requirements.requirement: Implement an incident response platform that supports the full breach response lifecycle: detection, triage, containment, investigation, remediation, notification, and closure with workflow automation and audit trails.
requirements.rationale: Manual breach response is slow and error-prone. A platform ensures consistent, trackable execution of the response plan.

governance.summary: AI Generated: The breach response plan must be governed with clear ownership, regular testing, and update cycles.

requirements.requirement: Assign clear ownership of the breach response plan and establish a governance process for plan maintenance, testing, and post-incident improvement.
requirements.rationale: Plans without ownership and testing become stale. Governance ensures the plan remains current and effective.
requirements.frequency: Annual governance review; plan tested annually

business.summary: AI Generated: The breach response plan must include escalation procedures and clear responsibility lines for containment, remediation, and recurrence prevention.

requirements.requirement: Define escalation procedures within the breach response plan specifying severity levels, escalation triggers, escalation targets (by role), and maximum response timeframes for each level.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.2 mandates escalation procedures. Clear escalation ensures the right people are engaged at the right time.

requirements.requirement: Define a clear RACI matrix for breach response activities covering containment, remediation, investigation, notification, and recurrence prevention.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.2 requires clear lines of responsibility. A RACI matrix eliminates ambiguity during high-pressure breach scenarios.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Escalation workflows must be automated to ensure timeliness.

requirements.requirement: Implement automated escalation workflows in the incident management system that trigger notifications based on severity, time elapsed, and response status.
requirements.rationale: Automated escalation prevents delays in engaging appropriate personnel during breach response.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Escalation procedures must be tested and validated through regular exercises.

requirements.requirement: Test escalation procedures through tabletop exercises and breach simulations at least annually, documenting outcomes and improvements.
requirements.rationale: Untested escalation procedures may fail under pressure. Regular testing validates effectiveness.

business.summary: AI Generated: All staff must understand escalation procedures, and relevant staff must be trained to take effective remedial actions during breaches to protect affected customers.

requirements.requirement: Train all staff on breach escalation procedures and provide specialised incident response training to staff with specific breach response roles.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.3 requires universal understanding of escalation and specific training for responders. Both general awareness and specialist capability are needed.
requirements.frequency: Annual training; refreshers after significant incidents

technology.summary: AI Generated: Training should include practical exercises using the incident response platform.

requirements.requirement: Include hands-on exercises using the incident response platform in specialised breach response training, ensuring responders are proficient with the tools.
requirements.rationale: Tool proficiency under pressure requires practice. Hands-on training builds muscle memory for effective response.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Training completion must be tracked and response capability validated.

requirements.requirement: Track escalation procedure training completion for all staff and specialised response training for designated responders, escalating gaps in coverage.
requirements.rationale: Universal understanding requires universal training. Tracking ensures no gaps exist.

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must have mechanisms to detect breaches, including those surfaced through customer complaints, and must investigate complaints promptly and properly.

requirements.requirement: Establish breach identification mechanisms across all channels including technical monitoring systems, internal reporting channels, and customer complaint analysis workflows.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.4 requires breach identification mechanisms including those arising from complaints. Multi-channel detection maximises breach discovery.

requirements.requirement: Integrate customer complaint handling processes with breach identification workflows so that complaints potentially indicative of breaches are flagged and investigated promptly.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.4 specifically includes customer complaints as a breach identification source. Integration prevents complaints falling through cracks.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technical detection mechanisms must complement human reporting channels.

requirements.requirement: Deploy automated breach detection systems (SIEM, DLP, anomaly detection) complemented by easy-to-use internal reporting channels (hotline, web form, email) for staff and customer-reported breaches.
requirements.rationale: Comprehensive breach identification requires both automated detection and accessible human reporting channels.

governance.summary: AI Generated: All breach identification sources must be monitored and complaint investigations tracked.

requirements.requirement: Track complaint investigation timeliness and outcomes, reporting trends and systemic issues to senior management.
requirements.rationale: Prompt and proper investigation of complaints is a regulatory requirement. Tracking ensures timeliness.

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must act immediately to contain any customer information breach through appropriate mitigation measures.

requirements.requirement: Define and maintain pre-approved containment playbooks for common breach scenarios enabling immediate containment without waiting for management approval.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.5 requires immediate containment. Pre-approved playbooks eliminate approval delays during the critical initial response window.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technical containment capabilities must support immediate response.

requirements.requirement: Maintain immediate containment capabilities including network isolation, account suspension, system shutdown, and data access revocation, executable within minutes of breach confirmation.
requirements.rationale: Immediate containment requires pre-configured technical capabilities. Delays in containment increase breach impact.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Containment response times must be measured and reported.

requirements.requirement: Measure and report containment response times for all breach incidents, with targets for time-to-contain and escalation for breaches exceeding target times.
requirements.rationale: Measuring response times drives continuous improvement in containment speed.

business.summary: AI Generated: When a breach at one FSP involves customer data belonging to another FSP, the breached FSP must promptly notify the other FSP for timely remedial actions.

requirements.requirement: Establish a cross-FSP breach notification procedure specifying how to identify when a breach involves another FSP's customer data, notification channels, required content, and timeframes.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.6 requires prompt notification to affected FSPs. A predefined procedure ensures this happens without delay.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Systems must support identification of cross-FSP data in breach scenarios.

requirements.requirement: Ensure data classification and mapping enables identification of customer information belonging to other FSPs within the FSP's systems, supporting cross-FSP breach impact assessment.
requirements.rationale: You cannot notify another FSP if you cannot identify their data in a breach. Data classification is foundational.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Cross-FSP notification obligations must be incorporated into the breach response governance framework.

requirements.requirement: Include cross-FSP notification as a mandatory step in the breach response checklist, with documentation of notifications sent and received.
requirements.rationale: Cross-FSP notification is a regulatory obligation that must not be overlooked during breach response.
requirements.frequency: Per breach event; process reviewed annually

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must assess breach impact considering: accidental vs intentional nature, data type and sensitivity, number of affected customers, who data was exposed to, and likelihood of fraudulent or harmful use.

requirements.requirement: Develop a Breach Impact Assessment Framework with mandatory assessment criteria covering all five factors specified: (a) intent, (b) data type/sensitivity, (c) affected customer count, (d) exposure recipients, and (e) fraud/harm likelihood.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.7 mandates assessment against five specific criteria. A structured framework ensures comprehensive and consistent impact assessment.
requirements.frequency: Framework reviewed annually; applied per incident

technology.summary: AI Generated: Systems must support rapid impact assessment data gathering.

requirements.requirement: Implement tools and queries that enable rapid determination of breach scope: affected data types, record counts, customer counts, and data recipients, within hours of breach discovery.
requirements.rationale: Impact assessment depends on scope determination. Rapid technical analysis supports timely assessment and notification decisions.
requirements.frequency: Tools maintained continuously; tested in exercises

governance.summary: AI Generated: Impact assessments must be documented and reviewed for consistency.

requirements.requirement: Document all breach impact assessments using the standardised framework and review assessment consistency across incidents annually.
requirements.rationale: Documented assessments provide regulatory evidence and enable consistency review.

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must immediately notify BNM upon discovering a breach that: (a) poses reputational risk or threatens public confidence, (b) causes or may cause significant harm to customers, or (c) affects or may affect a large number of customers (significant scale).

requirements.requirement: Establish a BNM Notification Decision Protocol defining the three mandatory trigger criteria and the process for making rapid notification decisions, with pre-drafted notification templates.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.8 requires immediate notification. Pre-defined criteria and templates enable rapid decision-making and notification execution.
requirements.frequency: Protocol reviewed annually; executed per qualifying breach

requirements.requirement: Designate and train staff authorised to make BNM notification decisions, ensuring 24/7 availability of a decision-maker for after-hours breaches.
requirements.rationale: Immediate notification requires available decision-makers. 24/7 availability ensures no delay in notification regardless of when a breach is discovered.
requirements.frequency: Designations reviewed annually; availability confirmed quarterly

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technology must support rapid breach qualification against BNM notification criteria.

requirements.requirement: Integrate BNM notification trigger assessment into the incident management workflow, with automated checks against the three notification criteria and escalation when thresholds are likely met.
requirements.rationale: Automated trigger assessment reduces the risk of missing a notification obligation during a complex breach response.

governance.summary: AI Generated: BNM notification decisions must be documented and auditable.

requirements.requirement: Document all BNM notification decisions (both to notify and decisions that notification was not required) with supporting rationale, for regulatory audit purposes.
requirements.rationale: Both notification and non-notification decisions must be defensible. Documentation provides the evidence base.

business.summary: AI Generated: A breach poses reputational risk when it involves disclosure to suspected criminals, public circulation via social media, or concerns a public figure or is likely to attract media attention.

requirements.requirement: Incorporate the specific reputational risk indicators from clause 11.9 into the BNM notification assessment criteria: (a) criminal involvement, (b) public/social media circulation, and (c) public figure involvement or media attention likelihood.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.9 defines specific reputational risk triggers. These must be explicitly checked during breach assessment.
requirements.frequency: Per breach assessment; criteria reviewed annually

technology.summary: AI Generated: Media and social media monitoring should support reputational risk assessment.

requirements.requirement: Implement social media and media monitoring capabilities that can rapidly identify whether breach-related information is circulating publicly, supporting the reputational risk assessment.
requirements.rationale: Real-time media monitoring enables rapid detection of public exposure, triggering BNM notification obligations.
requirements.frequency: Activated per breach event; tools maintained continuously

governance.summary: AI Generated: Reputational risk assessments must be documented as part of the BNM notification decision.

requirements.requirement: Document reputational risk assessment findings for each breach, recording the evaluation against each specified indicator.
requirements.rationale: Documentation supports the notification decision and provides regulatory evidence.

business.summary: AI Generated: Significant harm is indicated when a breach risks: financial loss, property damage, reputational harm, credit record impact, safety threats, illegal misuse, identity theft/fraud, or involves sensitive customer information.

requirements.requirement: Incorporate all significant harm indicators from clause 11.10 into the breach impact assessment framework: (a) financial loss/property damage/reputation/credit/safety, (b) illegal misuse, (c) identity theft/fraud, and (d) sensitive information involvement.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.10 defines what constitutes significant harm. Each indicator must be explicitly assessed for every breach.
requirements.frequency: Per breach assessment; criteria reviewed annually

technology.summary: AI Generated: Data classification must enable rapid identification of sensitive customer information in breach scope.

requirements.requirement: Maintain data classification that tags sensitive customer information categories (security credentials, biometric data, health information) enabling rapid identification of sensitive data in breach scope assessment.
requirements.rationale: Sensitive information involvement is a significant harm indicator. Classification enables rapid scope qualification.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Significant harm assessments must be formally documented.

requirements.requirement: Document the significant harm assessment for each breach, recording the evaluation against each specified indicator and the overall determination.
requirements.rationale: The significant harm determination drives BNM notification and customer notification obligations. Documentation must be thorough.

business.summary: AI Generated: A breach is of significant scale if the number of affected customers exceeds 1,000.

requirements.requirement: Establish a 1,000-customer threshold in breach assessment procedures as the trigger for significant scale classification, with rapid customer count estimation procedures.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.11 defines the 1,000-customer threshold. Clear operational procedures ensure this is quickly assessed.
requirements.frequency: Procedure reviewed annually; applied per breach

technology.summary: AI Generated: Systems must support rapid affected customer counting.

requirements.requirement: Maintain pre-built queries and tools that can rapidly estimate and then precisely count the number of customers affected by a breach, across all impacted systems.
requirements.rationale: The 1,000-customer threshold requires rapid counting capability. Pre-built queries enable fast estimation.
requirements.frequency: Tools maintained continuously; tested in exercises

governance.summary: AI Generated: Customer count determinations must be documented and justified.

requirements.requirement: Document the customer count methodology and results for each breach, including initial estimates and final counts, with audit trail showing the basis for each figure.
requirements.rationale: The customer count directly affects regulatory notification obligations. Methodology must be transparent and defensible.

business.summary: AI Generated: When a breach reaches significant scale (over 1,000 customers), FSPs must assess potential impact and take actions to avoid or reduce harm to affected customers.

requirements.requirement: Develop pre-defined harm reduction playbooks for significant-scale breaches, including proactive customer protection measures such as account monitoring enhancement, credential resets, and fraud alert activation.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.12 requires appropriate actions to reduce harm. Pre-defined playbooks enable rapid customer protection at scale.
requirements.frequency: Annual review; executed per qualifying breach

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technical capabilities must support mass customer protection actions.

requirements.requirement: Maintain capabilities for mass customer protection actions including bulk credential resets, account monitoring enhancement, transaction alerts, and temporary access restrictions deployable at scale.
requirements.rationale: Significant-scale breaches require protection measures that can be applied to thousands of customers rapidly.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Harm reduction actions must be documented and reported.

requirements.requirement: Document all harm reduction actions taken for significant-scale breaches, including timing, scope, and effectiveness assessment.
requirements.rationale: Regulatory review will assess the adequacy of harm reduction measures. Documentation provides the evidence base.

business.summary: AI Generated: The assessment of significant harm must be confirmed by the designated senior officer appointed under paragraph 9.2.

requirements.requirement: Require the Designated Officer under paragraph 9.2 to formally confirm or approve all significant harm assessments before BNM notification decisions are finalised.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.13 mandates senior officer confirmation. This ensures appropriate seniority in the notification decision chain.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Workflow systems must route significant harm assessments to the Designated Officer for approval.

requirements.requirement: Configure incident management workflows to require Designated Officer sign-off on significant harm assessments before BNM notification can proceed.
requirements.rationale: Workflow enforcement ensures the approval step cannot be bypassed.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Designated Officer sign-off must be documented and auditable.

requirements.requirement: Maintain records of all Designated Officer significant harm assessment confirmations, including timestamp, assessment reviewed, and confirmation decision.
requirements.rationale: Auditable sign-off records demonstrate compliance with the senior officer confirmation requirement.

business.summary: AI Generated: When breaches become public via social media or other channels, FSPs must manage reputational risk and ensure immediate BNM notification.

requirements.requirement: Develop a Reputational Risk Management Playbook for public breaches, including media response protocols, social media management, and coordinated BNM notification.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.14 requires effective reputational risk management for public breaches. Pre-planned responses prevent reactive, inconsistent messaging.
requirements.frequency: Annual review; executed per public breach event

technology.summary: AI Generated: Social media monitoring and crisis communication tools must support rapid response.

requirements.requirement: Deploy social media monitoring and crisis communication tools that alert the FSP when breach-related information appears publicly and support coordinated response.
requirements.rationale: Early detection of public exposure enables proactive management rather than reactive damage control.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Public breach response actions must be coordinated through a defined crisis management structure.

requirements.requirement: Establish a crisis management team structure for public breach scenarios with defined roles, communication protocols, and decision-making authority.
requirements.rationale: Public breaches require coordinated multi-functional response. A predefined structure enables effective coordination.

business.summary: AI Generated: If a breach appears to involve fraud, criminal activity, or potential identity theft, FSPs must also notify relevant law enforcement as soon as practicable.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Law Enforcement Notification Procedure specifying the criteria for law enforcement engagement, designated contact points, and the process for making and documenting notifications.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.15 requires law enforcement notification for criminal breaches. Pre-defined procedures ensure timely notification without compromising evidence.
requirements.frequency: Annual review; executed per qualifying breach

technology.summary: AI Generated: Evidence preservation must support potential law enforcement investigations.

requirements.requirement: Implement forensic evidence preservation procedures that maintain chain of custody and evidentiary integrity for potential law enforcement proceedings.
requirements.rationale: Law enforcement referrals require evidence that meets legal standards. Forensic preservation ensures evidence admissibility.
requirements.frequency: Procedures maintained continuously; applied per criminal breach

governance.summary: AI Generated: Law enforcement notification decisions must be governed and documented.

requirements.requirement: Document all law enforcement notification decisions including rationale and maintain records of all interactions with law enforcement during breach investigations.
requirements.rationale: Documentation supports regulatory review and provides an audit trail for governance oversight.

business.summary: AI Generated: BNM notification must include at minimum: breach description with data types affected, number of affected customers, actual and potential consequences, and measures taken or planned.

requirements.requirement: Create and maintain a BNM Breach Notification Template covering all five mandatory content elements: (a) breach description and data types, (b) affected customer count, (c) actual consequences, (d) potential consequences, and (e) measures taken or planned.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.16 specifies five mandatory notification elements. A template ensures completeness under time pressure.
requirements.frequency: Template reviewed annually; used per notification

technology.summary: AI Generated: Systems must support rapid compilation of notification data.

requirements.requirement: Enable rapid extraction of breach notification data from incident management and monitoring systems, including affected data types, record counts, and containment measures taken.
requirements.rationale: Notification timeliness depends on data availability. Integrated systems reduce compilation time.

governance.summary: AI Generated: All BNM notifications must be quality-reviewed before submission.

requirements.requirement: Establish a quality review process for BNM notifications ensuring all five mandatory elements are addressed before submission, with sign-off by the Designated Officer.
requirements.rationale: Incomplete notifications may result in regulatory follow-up and demonstrate inadequate processes.

business.summary: AI Generated: If complete information is unavailable when notifying BNM, FSPs must provide whatever is available immediately, submit estimated affected customer numbers, and provide complete information without undue delay.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Phased Notification Procedure for BNM notifications where complete information is unavailable, defining initial notification content, estimation methodology for affected customer counts, and follow-up information submission timelines.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.17 creates a phased notification framework. Procedures must support immediate partial notification followed by complete notification.
requirements.frequency: Procedure reviewed annually; applied per event

technology.summary: AI Generated: Estimation tools must support rapid customer count approximation when exact figures are unavailable.

requirements.requirement: Develop customer impact estimation tools and methodologies that can produce defensible estimates when exact counts are not yet available, with clear assumptions documentation.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.17(b) requires estimated numbers based on initial assessment. Estimation tools enable compliance when exact counts are unavailable.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Phased notifications must be tracked to ensure complete information is submitted without undue delay.

requirements.requirement: Track all phased BNM notifications to ensure complete information is submitted without undue delay, with escalation if follow-up submissions are overdue.
requirements.rationale: Incomplete initial notifications must be completed. Tracking prevents follow-up submissions from being overlooked.

11.18.subsection: C: Submission of detailed investigation report

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must investigate breach root causes and determine remedial actions. The investigation must be conducted by a competent party and overseen by a party independent of the business unit where the breach occurred.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Breach Investigation Framework requiring root cause analysis, remediation planning, competent investigation parties, and independent oversight separate from the affected business unit.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.18 mandates competent investigation with independent oversight. This ensures objectivity and thoroughness.
requirements.frequency: Framework reviewed annually; applied per breach

technology.summary: AI Generated: Forensic investigation tools and evidence management capabilities must support competent investigations.

requirements.requirement: Maintain forensic investigation capabilities including log analysis, data flow reconstruction, timeline analysis, and evidence management tools to support competent breach investigations.
requirements.rationale: Competent investigation requires proper tools. Forensic capabilities enable thorough root cause analysis.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Investigation independence must be verified and documented.

requirements.requirement: Verify and document the independence of the investigation oversight party from the affected business unit for each breach investigation.
requirements.rationale: Independence is a regulatory requirement. Verification ensures it is genuine, not nominal.

11.19.subsection: C: Submission of detailed investigation report

business.summary: AI Generated: Investigations must be completed within three months of detecting a breach, and a detailed investigation report must be tabled to the Board.

requirements.requirement: Enforce a three-month maximum investigation timeline from breach detection to Board tabling, with milestone tracking and escalation for investigations at risk of exceeding the deadline.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.19 mandates a three-month completion timeline. Milestone tracking ensures timely completion.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Investigation project management tools must track timelines and milestones.

requirements.requirement: Use investigation management tools that track investigation milestones against the three-month deadline with automated alerts for approaching deadlines.
requirements.rationale: Timeline tracking ensures the regulatory deadline is met and early warning is provided for at-risk investigations.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Investigation timelines must be monitored and reported to the Board.

requirements.requirement: Report investigation status and timeline compliance to the Board, with immediate escalation if any investigation risks exceeding the three-month deadline.
requirements.rationale: Board awareness of investigation progress is essential given the Board tabling requirement.
requirements.frequency: Monthly status updates; Board tabling per completion

11.20.subsection: C: Submission of detailed investigation report

business.summary: AI Generated: The detailed investigation report and Appendix 1 must be submitted to BNM within one working day of Board tabling for breaches causing significant harm, exceeding 1,000 customers, or involving deliberate unauthorised disclosure.

requirements.requirement: Establish a BNM Investigation Report Submission Process with a one-working-day target from Board tabling, including pre-preparation of Appendix 1 and submission logistics to ensure the deadline is met.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.20 mandates one-working-day submission. Pre-preparation is essential given the tight timeline.
requirements.frequency: Process reviewed annually; executed per qualifying breach

technology.summary: AI Generated: Report preparation tools must support rapid compilation and submission.

requirements.requirement: Maintain templates and data extraction capabilities that enable rapid compilation of the detailed investigation report and Appendix 1 for BNM submission.
requirements.rationale: One-working-day submission requires efficient report compilation. Templates and data tools reduce preparation time.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Submission timeliness must be tracked and verified.

requirements.requirement: Track submission dates relative to Board tabling dates for all qualifying breaches, ensuring compliance with the one-working-day requirement.
requirements.rationale: Late submission represents regulatory non-compliance. Tracking ensures the obligation is met.

11.21.subsection: C: Submission of detailed investigation report

business.summary: AI Generated: The investigation report must be signed off by the designated senior officer and submitted to the appropriate BNM department (Consumer and Market Conduct Department, or Payment Services Oversight Department for payment entities).

requirements.requirement: Obtain Designated Officer sign-off on all investigation reports before submission to BNM, and ensure submission is directed to the correct BNM department based on the FSP's licence type.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.21 requires designated officer sign-off and specifies the correct submission addresses. Incorrect routing delays regulatory processing.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Document management systems must support sign-off workflows and submission tracking.

requirements.requirement: Implement digital sign-off workflows for investigation reports with audit trails confirming the Designated Officer's approval before submission.
