requirements.rationale: Digital sign-off provides verifiable evidence of the required approval and supports rapid processing.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Sign-off and submission records must be maintained for audit purposes.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a submission register recording all investigation reports submitted to BNM, including sign-off date, submission date, BNM department, and delivery confirmation.
requirements.rationale: A submission register provides the audit trail demonstrating compliance with submission requirements.

11.22.subsection: C: Submission of detailed investigation report

business.summary: AI Generated: When a breach at one FSP involves another FSP's customer data, the FSP where the breach occurred bears full responsibility for investigation and reporting.

requirements.requirement: Include cross-FSP breach investigation responsibility in the breach response framework, ensuring the FSP where the breach occurred takes ownership of all investigation and reporting actions regardless of data ownership.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.22 assigns investigation responsibility to the breached FSP. This must be operationally clear to prevent responsibility disputes.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Investigation capabilities must cover data belonging to other FSPs within the FSP's systems.

requirements.requirement: Ensure investigation tools and processes can identify and analyse breaches affecting other FSPs' customer data within the FSP's systems.
requirements.rationale: Cross-FSP investigations require the ability to identify and scope data belonging to partner FSPs.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Cross-FSP investigation responsibilities must be documented in inter-FSP agreements.

requirements.requirement: Include investigation responsibility and cooperation obligations in inter-FSP data sharing agreements, aligning with clause 11.22 requirements.
requirements.rationale: Contractual clarity prevents disputes during breach scenarios where multiple FSPs are involved.

11.23.subsection: C: Submission of detailed investigation report

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must maintain a breach register recording all customer information breaches, root causes, remedial actions, and lessons learnt.

requirements.requirement: Establish and maintain a comprehensive Breach Register recording every customer information breach including: date, description, root cause, affected systems/data, customer impact, remedial actions taken, lessons learnt, and preventive measures implemented.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.23 mandates a breach register with specific content requirements. A comprehensive register supports trend analysis and regulatory review.

technology.summary: AI Generated: The breach register should be maintained in a secure, auditable system.

requirements.requirement: Implement a secure, access-controlled breach register system with audit trail capabilities, supporting trend analysis and reporting.
requirements.rationale: The breach register contains sensitive information requiring protection. Audit trails ensure register integrity.

governance.summary: AI Generated: The breach register must be regularly reviewed for trends and completeness.

requirements.requirement: Review the breach register quarterly for completeness, trend analysis, and lessons learnt, reporting findings to senior management.
requirements.rationale: A register is only valuable if reviewed. Regular analysis identifies patterns and informs preventive measures.

11.24.subsection: C: Submission of detailed investigation report

business.summary: AI Generated: Investigation reports for all breach incidents must be retained for at least seven years from the date tabled to the Board.

requirements.requirement: Establish a seven-year minimum retention policy for all breach investigation reports, measured from the date the report was tabled to the Board.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.24 mandates seven-year retention. Clear retention policy ensures compliance and prevents premature disposal.
requirements.frequency: Policy reviewed annually; retention verified continuously

technology.summary: AI Generated: Document management systems must enforce the seven-year retention requirement.

requirements.requirement: Configure document management systems to enforce seven-year retention of breach investigation reports with retention hold dates tied to Board tabling dates and prevent premature deletion.
requirements.rationale: Technical enforcement prevents accidental or premature disposal of investigation reports.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Retention compliance must be verified periodically.

requirements.requirement: Conduct annual audits of breach investigation report retention to verify all reports are retained for the required seven-year period.
requirements.rationale: Retention obligations require periodic verification to ensure compliance over the long retention period.

business.summary: AI Generated: When a breach causes or may cause significant harm, FSPs must notify affected customers without undue delay after BNM notification.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Customer Notification Decision and Execution Process triggered by significant harm determination, with defined timelines for notification following BNM notification, and pre-approved notification templates.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.25 requires notification without undue delay. Pre-prepared processes and templates enable rapid customer notification.
requirements.frequency: Process reviewed annually; executed per qualifying breach

technology.summary: AI Generated: Mass customer notification capabilities must support rapid, multi-channel delivery.

requirements.requirement: Maintain mass customer notification capabilities supporting multiple channels (email, SMS, push notification, mail) with personalisation, delivery tracking, and scalability for large-scale breaches.
requirements.rationale: Notification without undue delay at scale requires pre-built, tested mass communication capabilities.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Notification timing must be tracked and documented.

requirements.requirement: Track and document the timeline from BNM notification to customer notification for each qualifying breach, with justification for any delays.
requirements.rationale: Without undue delay requires demonstrable timeliness. Timeline documentation provides evidence of compliance.

business.summary: AI Generated: Any delay in notifying affected customers must not result in further harm to those customers.

requirements.requirement: Implement interim protective measures for affected customers during any notification delay period to prevent further harm, such as enhanced account monitoring or preventive fraud controls.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.26 prohibits delays that cause further harm. Interim measures protect customers while notification is being prepared.
requirements.frequency: Per breach event where notification is delayed

technology.summary: AI Generated: Interim protection measures must be deployable before formal customer notification.

requirements.requirement: Maintain the ability to deploy protective controls (enhanced monitoring, transaction limits, fraud alerts) for affected customer accounts before formal notification is sent.
requirements.rationale: Technical protection can be applied faster than notification can be prepared and delivered, reducing the harm window.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Delay justification and interim protective measures must be documented.

requirements.requirement: Document any notification delay, the justification, interim protective measures applied, and assessment that the delay does not cause further harm.
requirements.rationale: Regulatory scrutiny of notification delays requires documented justification and evidence of harm prevention.

business.summary: AI Generated: Customer notifications must include: breach description, potential consequences, protective steps for customers, FSP remedial measures, and contact details for further assistance.

requirements.requirement: Create customer notification templates that include all five mandatory elements: (a) breach description, (b) potential consequences, (c) protective steps customers should take, (d) FSP remedial measures, and (e) contact details for assistance.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.27 specifies five mandatory notification elements. Templates ensure completeness under time pressure.
requirements.frequency: Templates reviewed annually; used per notification

technology.summary: AI Generated: Notification delivery systems must support personalised, content-rich notifications.

requirements.requirement: Configure notification systems to deliver personalised breach notifications containing all five mandatory elements, with dynamic content based on the specific breach characteristics.
requirements.rationale: Different breaches require different protective advice. Dynamic content enables relevant, personalised notifications.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Notification content must be reviewed for completeness before delivery.

requirements.requirement: Establish a notification content review process ensuring all five mandatory elements are present and accurate before customer notification delivery.
requirements.rationale: Incomplete notifications fail to meet regulatory requirements and may not adequately protect customers.

business.summary: AI Generated: Notifications must be clear, in plain language, and draw customers' attention to protective steps they should take.

requirements.requirement: Ensure all customer breach notifications are written in plain, non-technical language, with protective steps prominently highlighted and clearly actionable.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.28 requires plain language and emphasis on protective steps. Notifications that customers cannot understand fail their purpose.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Notification formatting should emphasise protective steps visually.

requirements.requirement: Design notification templates with clear visual hierarchy that draws attention to protective steps customers should take, tested for readability across all delivery channels.
requirements.rationale: Visual design affects comprehension. Protective steps must be unmissable in the notification.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Notification clarity should be validated through readability testing.

requirements.requirement: Test notification templates for readability and comprehension with representative customer samples before finalising.
requirements.rationale: Plain language is subjective. Testing with actual readers validates that notifications achieve their communication purpose.
requirements.frequency: Upon template creation or major revision

business.summary: AI Generated: Affected customers must be notified directly. In exceptional cases of disproportionate effort, FSPs may use public announcements and prominent branch/website notices if sufficient.

requirements.requirement: Default to direct individual notification for all affected customers, using appropriate channels (registered mail, email, phone, SMS, push notification, in-app notification).
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.29 mandates direct notification as the default. Alternative methods are only for exceptional circumstances.

requirements.requirement: Define criteria for determining when direct notification entails disproportionate effort, with a documented approval process by senior management for switching to public announcement notification.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.29 limits the public announcement alternative to exceptional circumstances. Criteria and approval prevent overuse of this exception.
requirements.frequency: Criteria reviewed annually; applied per exception

technology.summary: AI Generated: Multi-channel notification capabilities must support direct customer contact.

requirements.requirement: Maintain multi-channel direct notification capabilities (email, SMS, push notification, in-app, mail) with customer contact information accuracy verified regularly.
requirements.rationale: Direct notification requires accurate contact information and working delivery channels. Regular verification ensures deliverability.
requirements.frequency: Contact accuracy verified annually; channels tested quarterly

governance.summary: AI Generated: Decisions to use alternative notification methods must be documented and justified.

requirements.requirement: Document any decision to use public announcement notification instead of direct notification, including the disproportionate effort justification and senior management approval.
requirements.rationale: Alternative methods require documented justification for regulatory scrutiny.

business.summary: AI Generated: If specific affected customers cannot be identified, FSPs must first issue a general public announcement. Once identified, they must be notified directly as soon as feasible.

requirements.requirement: Develop a two-stage notification procedure for breaches where affected customers cannot initially be identified: Stage 1 - immediate public announcement and branch/website notices; Stage 2 - direct individual notification once customers are identified.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.30 defines a two-stage notification approach for unknown-victim breaches. Both stages must be planned and executed.
requirements.frequency: Procedure reviewed annually; executed per qualifying event

technology.summary: AI Generated: Customer identification tools must support progressive identification of affected customers.

requirements.requirement: Implement data analysis capabilities that progressively identify specific affected customers as investigation proceeds, triggering Stage 2 direct notifications.
requirements.rationale: Progressive identification requires analytical tools that can correlate breach scope with customer records.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Both notification stages must be tracked to completion.

requirements.requirement: Track progression from general public announcement to direct customer notification, ensuring all eventually-identified customers receive direct notification.
requirements.rationale: The general announcement does not discharge the direct notification obligation. Tracking ensures all customers are eventually directly notified.
requirements.frequency: Per event; tracked until all identified customers notified

business.summary: AI Generated: Staff must be trained to handle queries from affected customers and provide assistance on protective measures against potential breach consequences.

requirements.requirement: Train front-line and customer-facing staff to handle breach-related customer queries, including providing guidance on protective measures and escalating complex queries to specialist teams.
requirements.rationale: Clause 11.31 requires staff training for post-breach customer support. Affected customers will contact the FSP seeking assistance.
requirements.frequency: Training included in annual programme; refreshed after significant breaches

requirements.requirement: Prepare breach-specific customer query response guides and FAQs for front-line staff when a significant breach occurs, with approved responses for common questions.
requirements.rationale: Staff need specific guidance for each breach scenario. Generic training alone is insufficient for effective customer support.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Customer support systems must be equipped to handle breach-related queries efficiently.

requirements.requirement: Configure customer support systems with breach-specific FAQs, response scripts, and escalation workflows that enable efficient handling of breach-related customer queries.
requirements.rationale: Configured systems enable consistent, accurate responses and ensure queries are handled efficiently during high-volume periods.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Customer query handling effectiveness must be monitored during breach response.

requirements.requirement: Monitor customer query volumes, response times, and resolution rates during breach notification periods, reporting to senior management.
requirements.rationale: Monitoring ensures affected customers receive adequate assistance and identifies capacity constraints.
requirements.frequency: Daily during active breach response; summary post-incident

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must actively monitor and manage risks associated with outsourced service providers that handle customer information.

requirements.requirement: Establish an OSP Risk Monitoring Programme that identifies, assesses, and monitors risks arising from all outsourced service providers handling customer information.
requirements.rationale: Clause 12.1 mandates risk monitoring for OSPs. A structured programme ensures systematic coverage of all OSP relationships.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technology must support monitoring of OSP-related risks and activities.

requirements.requirement: Implement monitoring capabilities for OSP activities involving customer information, including access logging, data transfer monitoring, and SLA performance tracking.
requirements.rationale: Risk monitoring requires visibility into OSP activities. Technical monitoring provides objective data for risk assessment.

governance.summary: AI Generated: OSP risk monitoring must be governed within the broader risk management framework.

requirements.requirement: Integrate OSP risk monitoring into the enterprise risk management framework with defined reporting, escalation, and remediation processes.
requirements.rationale: OSP risks must receive governance attention proportionate to their significance.

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must perform thorough due diligence when selecting OSPs with access to customer information, to understand risk levels and determine appropriate ongoing monitoring.

requirements.requirement: Conduct comprehensive due diligence on all prospective OSPs that will access, process, store, or dispose of customer information, covering security posture, financial stability, regulatory compliance, and track record.
requirements.rationale: Clause 12.2 mandates adequate due diligence. Pre-engagement assessment prevents onboarding of high-risk providers.
requirements.frequency: Per OSP selection; repeated at contract renewal

requirements.requirement: Use due diligence findings to determine a risk-based monitoring intensity level for each OSP, with higher-risk providers subject to more frequent and detailed monitoring.
requirements.rationale: Clause 12.2 requires assessments to determine appropriate monitoring. Risk-based monitoring optimises resource allocation.
requirements.frequency: Per OSP; monitoring levels reviewed annually

technology.summary: AI Generated: Due diligence should include technical security assessments of the OSP's environment.

requirements.requirement: Include technical security assessment in OSP due diligence covering the OSP's information security controls, encryption practices, access management, and incident response capabilities.
requirements.rationale: Technical assessment validates the OSP's ability to protect customer information within their environment.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Due diligence processes must be formalised and results documented.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a standardised OSP Due Diligence Framework with defined assessment criteria, scoring methodology, and approval thresholds for OSP engagement.
requirements.rationale: Standardised due diligence ensures consistent assessment quality and enables comparability across OSP candidates.

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must verify that OSPs maintain policies, procedures, and controls comparable to the FSP's own standards for safeguarding customer information.

requirements.requirement: Assess each OSP's customer information protection policies, procedures, and controls against the FSP's own standards, identifying gaps and requiring remediation to achieve comparable protection.
requirements.rationale: Clause 12.3 requires comparable standards. Assessment against the FSP's own benchmarks ensures equivalent protection.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technical controls at the OSP must be validated as comparable to the FSP's standards.

requirements.requirement: Conduct technical security assessments of OSP environments to verify controls are comparable to FSP standards, including encryption, access controls, monitoring, and incident detection.
requirements.rationale: Comparable standards require technical validation, not just policy review.
requirements.frequency: At engagement; reassessed at least biennially

governance.summary: AI Generated: Comparability assessments must be documented and gaps tracked.

requirements.requirement: Document comparability assessments for each OSP with gap analysis and remediation tracking until comparable standards are achieved.
requirements.rationale: Documentation provides evidence of compliance and enables tracking of gap closure.
requirements.frequency: Per assessment; gaps tracked to closure

business.summary: AI Generated: SLAs with OSPs must include nine mandatory obligations: (a) safeguard customer data, (b) maintain adequate policies, (c) vet personnel, (d) restrict access to SLA purposes, (e) ensure personnel comply with disclosure prohibitions, (f) investigate breaches, (g) report breaches within agreed timeframes, (h) destroy/return data upon termination, and (i) permit FSP audits. Existing SLAs must be amended to comply.

requirements.requirement: Review and amend all existing OSP SLAs to include all nine mandatory provisions specified in clause 12.4, and ensure all new SLAs include these provisions from inception.
requirements.rationale: Clause 12.4 mandates nine specific SLA provisions and requires amendment of existing SLAs, not just new ones. Immediate compliance action is needed.
requirements.frequency: Immediate review of existing SLAs; per new SLA

requirements.requirement: Develop a standard SLA Addendum Template incorporating all nine mandatory provisions that can be efficiently executed with existing OSPs.
requirements.rationale: An addendum template enables rapid compliance across the existing OSP portfolio without full contract renegotiation.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technical capabilities must support the SLA obligations including audit access, breach reporting integration, and data return/destruction.

requirements.requirement: Implement technical capabilities to support SLA obligations including: audit trail access for FSP audit rights, secure channels for breach reporting, and verified data destruction/return procedures.
requirements.rationale: SLA obligations require technical enablement. The FSP must be able to exercise its rights and verify OSP compliance.

governance.summary: AI Generated: SLA compliance with clause 12.4 must be tracked across the entire OSP portfolio.

requirements.requirement: Maintain an OSP SLA Compliance Register tracking the inclusion of all nine mandatory provisions across all OSP agreements, with remediation tracking for non-compliant SLAs.
requirements.rationale: Portfolio-wide tracking ensures no OSP SLA is overlooked and demonstrates systematic compliance to regulators.

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs are encouraged to clearly communicate to OSPs the specific control measures expected for processing, storing, transmitting, disposing, or destroying customer information.

requirements.requirement: Develop and communicate OSP Control Measure Specifications detailing the expected control measures for each stage of customer information handling: processing, storage, transmission, disposal, and destruction.
requirements.rationale: Clause 12.5 encourages clear expectations. Specific control specifications prevent ambiguity and enable compliance verification.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technical control specifications should be detailed enough for OSP implementation.

requirements.requirement: Define technical control specifications for OSPs covering encryption standards, access control requirements, monitoring expectations, and secure disposal methods.
requirements.rationale: Technical specifications enable OSPs to implement controls that meet the FSP's expectations without ambiguity.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Control expectations should be documented and acknowledged by OSPs.

requirements.requirement: Obtain documented acknowledgement from each OSP of the communicated control measure expectations.
requirements.rationale: Acknowledgement creates accountability and provides evidence that expectations were communicated.

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must ensure that OSPs execute a legally binding non-disclosure agreement covering the handling of customer information.

requirements.requirement: Require all OSPs to sign a legally binding Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) specifically covering customer information before any customer data is shared or access is granted.
requirements.rationale: Clause 12.6 mandates a binding NDA. The NDA creates legal recourse in case of unauthorised disclosure.
requirements.frequency: Per OSP engagement; NDA validity tracked

technology.summary: AI Generated: OSP access should not be provisioned until NDA execution is confirmed.

requirements.requirement: Implement access provisioning controls that prevent customer information access from being granted to OSPs until NDA execution is confirmed in the system.
requirements.rationale: Technical enforcement prevents access before legal protections are in place.

governance.summary: AI Generated: NDA execution must be tracked across the OSP portfolio.

requirements.requirement: Maintain a register of all OSP NDAs with execution dates, expiry dates, and coverage confirmation, ensuring no OSP operates without a current NDA.
requirements.rationale: NDA tracking ensures continuous legal protection across all OSP relationships.

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must ensure OSPs provide regular training to their personnel on customer information handling and review the adequacy and effectiveness of training.

requirements.requirement: Require OSPs to demonstrate regular training delivery to their personnel on customer information handling, with evidence of training content, frequency, and completion rates.
requirements.rationale: Clause 12.7 requires FSPs to ensure OSP training occurs. Evidence-based verification ensures the obligation is met.

requirements.requirement: Assess the adequacy and effectiveness of OSP training programmes through review of training content, assessment results, and incident correlation.
requirements.rationale: Clause 12.7 requires review of training adequacy and effectiveness, not just verification that training occurred.

technology.summary: AI Generated: OSP training evidence should be collected and tracked centrally.

requirements.requirement: Establish a mechanism to collect and track OSP training evidence centrally, including training completion certificates, assessment scores, and programme review reports.
requirements.rationale: Centralised tracking enables systematic verification of the OSP training obligation.

governance.summary: AI Generated: OSP training adequacy must be part of ongoing vendor governance.

requirements.requirement: Include OSP training adequacy assessment as a standing item in periodic vendor governance reviews.
requirements.rationale: Regular governance attention ensures training quality is maintained over time.

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs are encouraged to consider directly training OSP personnel to raise awareness of safeguarding customer information and contractual compliance.

requirements.requirement: Evaluate the benefit of providing direct training to OSP personnel on the FSP's customer information protection requirements and contractual obligations.
requirements.rationale: Clause 12.8 suggests direct training may enhance compliance. FSP-provided training ensures messaging consistency.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Training delivery platforms should support extension to OSP personnel where adopted.

requirements.requirement: If direct OSP training is adopted, extend LMS access to OSP personnel for FSP-specific customer information protection training modules.
requirements.rationale: LMS extension provides scalable, trackable training delivery to external personnel.
requirements.frequency: Per decision to adopt; maintained continuously if adopted

governance.summary: AI Generated: Direct training decisions should be documented with rationale.

requirements.requirement: Document the evaluation and decision regarding direct OSP training, including cost-benefit analysis.
requirements.rationale: Documentation supports governance oversight of vendor management decisions.

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must review their OSPs at least biennially to confirm the OSP is meeting contractual obligations to safeguard customer information.

requirements.requirement: Conduct a comprehensive review of each OSP at least once every two years, assessing fulfilment of all contractual obligations relating to customer information safeguarding.
requirements.rationale: Clause 12.9 mandates biennial review. Regular review detects control degradation and ensures ongoing compliance.
requirements.frequency: At least once every two years per OSP

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technical assessment should be included in OSP reviews.

requirements.requirement: Include technical security assessment (vulnerability assessment, control testing, access review) in biennial OSP reviews.
requirements.rationale: Technical assessment validates that OSP security controls remain effective over time.

governance.summary: AI Generated: OSP review schedules must be tracked to ensure the biennial requirement is met.

requirements.requirement: Maintain an OSP Review Schedule tracking the last and next review dates for each OSP, ensuring no gap exceeds two years.
requirements.rationale: Schedule tracking prevents inadvertent non-compliance with the biennial requirement.

business.summary: AI Generated: FSPs must maintain accurate and complete records, including audit trails, of all customer information shared with or provided to OSPs.

requirements.requirement: Establish procedures and systems for recording all customer information shared with or provided to OSPs, including data type, volume, date, purpose, recipient OSP, and authorising party.
requirements.rationale: Clause 12.10 mandates accurate and complete records. Comprehensive recording enables accountability and audit.
requirements.frequency: Per data sharing event; records reviewed quarterly

technology.summary: AI Generated: Data sharing with OSPs must be technically logged and tracked.

requirements.requirement: Implement automated data sharing logging that captures all customer information transfers to OSPs, including data type, volume, destination, timestamp, and initiating user.
requirements.rationale: Automated logging ensures complete and accurate records without reliance on manual processes.
requirements.frequency: Continuous; logs retained per retention policy

governance.summary: AI Generated: Data sharing records must be periodically reviewed for accuracy and completeness.

requirements.requirement: Conduct quarterly reviews of OSP data sharing records for accuracy, completeness, and authorisation, reporting anomalies to senior management.
requirements.rationale: Record accuracy must be validated. Quarterly review identifies gaps before they become systemic.

13.1.section: Conditions in relation to permitted disclosure

business.summary: AI Generated: Financial institutions, their directors and officers must comply with specified conditions when making permitted disclosures of customer information under Schedule 11 of the FSA and IFSA, and the Fourth Schedule of the DFIA.

requirements.requirement: Map all scenarios where customer information disclosure is permitted under Schedule 11 of the FSA/IFSA and the Fourth Schedule of the DFIA, and document the specific conditions that must be met for each disclosure type.
requirements.rationale: Clause 13.1 requires compliance with specified conditions for each disclosure type. A comprehensive mapping ensures no disclosure scenario is handled without proper conditions.
requirements.frequency: Annual review; updated upon legislative changes

requirements.requirement: Communicate permitted disclosure conditions to all directors, officers, and staff who may be involved in customer information disclosure decisions or execution.
requirements.rationale: Clause 13.1 applies to the financial institution, its directors and officers. All relevant personnel must understand the applicable conditions.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technology systems should support enforcement of disclosure conditions through workflow controls.

requirements.requirement: Implement disclosure management workflows that enforce condition compliance for permitted disclosures, preventing disclosures that have not been verified against applicable conditions.
requirements.rationale: Workflow enforcement reduces the risk of non-compliant disclosures by requiring condition verification before data release.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Permitted disclosure governance must ensure all disclosures comply with the conditions table.

requirements.requirement: Establish a Permitted Disclosure Governance Framework defining the approval process, condition verification, documentation requirements, and audit trail for each category of permitted disclosure.
requirements.rationale: Governance ensures that the conditions specified in the regulatory table are systematically applied to every disclosure.

13.2.section: Conditions in relation to permitted disclosure

business.summary: AI Generated: Items 5 (foreign supervisory authority), 6 (centralised group functions), and 7 (due diligence for M&A, capital raising, asset sales) in the disclosure conditions table do not apply to prescribed institutions.

requirements.requirement: If the FSP is a prescribed institution, document the exemption from items 5, 6, and 7 of the disclosure conditions table and ensure this is reflected in disclosure procedures and training.
requirements.rationale: Clause 13.2 provides a specific exemption for prescribed institutions. Accurate application prevents unnecessary compliance burden.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Disclosure workflow systems should reflect applicable exemptions based on institutional type.

requirements.requirement: Configure disclosure management workflows to reflect applicable exemptions based on the FSP's institutional classification.
requirements.rationale: System configuration should accurately reflect the regulatory requirements applicable to the specific FSP type.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Exemption applicability must be formally determined and documented.

requirements.requirement: Obtain and document a formal legal determination of the FSP's classification as a prescribed or non-prescribed institution for the purpose of clause 13.2 exemptions.
requirements.rationale: The exemption's applicability depends on institutional classification. Formal determination prevents incorrect application.
requirements.frequency: Upon initial determination; reviewed upon regulatory changes

13.3.section: Conditions in relation to permitted disclosure

business.summary: AI Generated: Financial institutions must establish adequate controls over permitted disclosures, including at minimum: verification of disclosure request authenticity, documentation requirements, and approval authority at senior levels.

requirements.requirement: Establish an authenticity verification process for all disclosure orders and requests, specifying the steps responsible officers must take to confirm the legitimacy of the request before any disclosure.
requirements.rationale: Clause 13.3(a) requires processes to verify authenticity. Fraudulent disclosure requests could lead to unauthorised data release.
requirements.frequency: Per disclosure request; process reviewed annually

requirements.requirement: Define comprehensive documentation requirements for all permitted disclosures, including the request, verification steps taken, data disclosed, recipient, date, and approval authority.
requirements.rationale: Clause 13.3(b) mandates documentation requirements. Complete records provide audit trail and regulatory evidence.

requirements.requirement: Establish authority levels for approving customer information disclosures at appropriately senior levels, with a documented authority matrix specifying who can approve each disclosure type.
requirements.rationale: Clause 13.3(c) requires authority levels at an appropriate senior level. Authority matrix prevents junior staff from authorising sensitive disclosures.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Technology must support disclosure control measures including verification, documentation, and approval workflows.

requirements.requirement: Implement a disclosure management system with built-in verification checklists, documentation capture, senior-level approval workflows, and audit trail for all permitted customer information disclosures.
requirements.rationale: A dedicated system ensures the three mandatory control elements are consistently applied to every disclosure.

governance.summary: AI Generated: Disclosure control adequacy must be periodically assessed and disclosures audited.

requirements.requirement: Conduct periodic audits of permitted disclosures to verify that authenticity verification, documentation, and appropriate authority approval were obtained for each disclosure.
requirements.rationale: Audit provides assurance that disclosure controls are functioning effectively in practice.

13.4.section: Conditions in relation to permitted disclosure

business.summary: AI Generated: Financial institutions seeking BNM approval for customer information disclosure under specific sections of the FSA, IFSA, or DFIA must complete and submit the Appendix V application form to BNM.

requirements.requirement: Establish a BNM Disclosure Approval Application Process including Appendix V form preparation, internal review, and submission procedures for disclosure requests requiring BNM approval.
requirements.rationale: Clause 13.4 specifies the application form and process for BNM-approved disclosures. A defined process ensures complete and timely applications.
requirements.frequency: Process reviewed annually; executed per application

requirements.requirement: Maintain a register of all BNM disclosure approval applications submitted, their status, and outcomes, ensuring no disclosure proceeds without the required BNM approval.
requirements.rationale: Tracking prevents disclosure before BNM approval is obtained and provides a complete record for compliance purposes.

technology.summary: AI Generated: Systems must enforce the requirement for BNM approval before regulated disclosures proceed.

requirements.requirement: Configure disclosure workflows to block disclosures requiring BNM approval until approval confirmation is recorded in the system.
requirements.rationale: Technical enforcement prevents premature disclosure before BNM approval is obtained.

governance.summary: AI Generated: BNM approval applications must be governed with appropriate oversight.

requirements.requirement: Require senior management review and approval of all BNM disclosure approval applications before submission, ensuring completeness and appropriateness.
requirements.rationale: Applications to BNM represent the FSP officially. Senior oversight ensures quality and appropriateness.
